briefs.title: Chief Election Officer to Be Appointed for 2026 Local Elections
briefs.summary: A Chief Election Officer will be appointed to manage the 2026 general local elections. This person will be in charge of running the election.

briefs.title: North Road BIA Renewal Approved Despite Petitions
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) despite some opposition. A BIA helps local businesses by promoting the area and managing improvements. Some property owners petitioned against the renewal, but the city decided to move forward.
briefs.action_required: No action is required unless you wish to get involved in the BIA.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority's Corporate Maintenance Matters Need Council Approval
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby needs to get Council approval for annual corporate maintenance matters related to the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA).

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Presents 2025 Financials, 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) presented its financial statements for 2025 and its operating budget for 2026. This shows how the BHA is managing money and planning for the future.

briefs.title: E-Bike, E-Scooter Share Bylaw Changes Proposed
briefs.summary: Proposed changes to bylaws may affect e-bike and electric kick scooter programs. These changes could impact how these services operate.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the proposed bylaw changes and their potential impact.

briefs.title: BC Housing Target Order: Track Progress and Report to Ministry
briefs.summary: The BC government requires municipalities with housing targets to track their progress. Municipalities must send a report to the BC Ministry of Housing, detailing how they are doing in meeting these targets. This helps the province monitor housing development across BC.
briefs.action_required: Present Housing Target Order and forward Progress Report to BC Ministry.

briefs.title: Building Benchmark BC: Authorize Participation in Year 7
briefs.summary: The Building Benchmark BC program helps building owners compare their energy and water use to similar buildings. This helps identify ways to save money and reduce environmental impact. The council is seeking authorization to participate in Year 7 of the program.
briefs.action_required: Review the Building Benchmark BC program details and decide whether to participate.

briefs.title: FortisBC Gas Line Work Approved: May Affect Pandora St, Gaglardi Way Businesses
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved FortisBC's request to install gas lines along Pandora Street and Gaglardi Way. This gives FortisBC the legal right to access the land for this work. Businesses in this area may experience disruptions during the gas line installation.
briefs.action_required: Check for updates from FortisBC about potential disruptions.

briefs.title: Modular Housing Four-Plex Development Contract Approved
briefs.summary: A contract has been approved for the development of a modular four-plex housing unit. This means the project can move forward with building the housing.

briefs.title: Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: The contract for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project has been approved. This means the project can move forward to replace the culvert (a structure that allows water to flow under a road or railway).

briefs.title: Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: A contract has been awarded for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving project. This means the project can move forward with construction.

briefs.title: Cellular Voice and Data Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: A contract change for cellular voice and data services has been approved. This means the business can now move forward with the updated terms for their phone and internet services.

briefs.title: HVAC System Maintenance & Repair: Approve Contract Change
briefs.summary: You need to approve a change to your Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system maintenance and repair contract. This ensures your system continues to run smoothly.
briefs.action_required: Approve the contract change for HVAC system maintenance and repair services.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Approve Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Renewal
briefs.summary: You need to approve the renewal of your Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription. This ensures your business can continue using the WebEx Contact Center.
briefs.action_required: Approve the contract renewal for your Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription.

briefs.title: Fire Protection System Contract Renewal Approved
briefs.summary: A contract renewal for fire protection systems and equipment services has been approved. This ensures your business's fire safety systems will continue to be maintained.
briefs.action_required: Review the renewed contract details for any changes.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Healthcare, Food Service

briefs.title: City to Review Financial Practices with External Expert
briefs.summary: The city will hire an outside expert to review its financial practices. This is to protect the city's money and lower financial risks. The review will help make sure the city is handling money responsibly.

briefs.title: Beresford Street Apartment Development Rezoning Application Moves Forward in Burnaby
briefs.summary: A rezoning application for a new apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street is moving to the next stage. Burnaby City Council will review the application at First and Second Reading. This is part of the process for getting approval to build the new development.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about public hearings if you have concerns about the development.

briefs.title: Developer Loses Security Deposit for Pier Project
briefs.summary: A developer forfeited the security (a sum of money put aside) for building an amenity pier. The local council will now decide what happens with the pier in the future. The developer failed to meet the terms of their agreement.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road Businesses to Fund Improvement Projects
briefs.summary: Burnaby has officially created the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA). Businesses in this area will now contribute funds to the BIA. The BIA will use these funds to improve and promote the North Road business district.
briefs.action_required: Understand that you will be required to pay a levy to the BIA.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Professional Services, Hotel & Accommodation
